RT_STOP

Daniel Arango

3/06/2026

#pDA102 is NAT10(-) #pDA98 is NAT10-WT #pDA154 is E389L (helicase mutant) #pDA156 is K290V (helicase mutant)

# ===============================
# Libraries
# ===============================
library(tidyverse)
library(rtracklayer)
library(GenomicRanges)

# ===============================
# BigWig files (your dataset)
# ===============================
# Make sure the file paths are correct
bw_files <- c(
  "SN2.bw","SN3.bw","SN4.bw","SN5.bw",
  "SL2.bw","SL4.bw","SL5.bw",
  "SL12.bw","SL13.bw","SL14.bw","SL15.bw"
)

# ===============================
# Replicate structure
# ===============================
samples <- tribble(
  ~Genotype, ~rep1, ~rep2, ~rep3,
  "pDA98",    "SN2.bw", "SL2.bw", "SL12.bw",
  "pDA102",   "SN3.bw", NA,       "SL13.bw",
  "pDA154",   "SN4.bw", "SL4.bw", "SL14.bw",
  "pDA156",   "SN5.bw", "SL5.bw", "SL15.bw"
)

# ===============================
# Genomic region
# ===============================
chr <- "GL000220.1"
start <- 110315
end   <- 110335

# ===============================
# Function to extract per-base RPKM
# ===============================
extract_signal <- function(bw_file){
  if(is.na(bw_file)) return(NULL)
  
  gr <- GRanges(chr, IRanges(start, end))
  
  # Import as NumericList (per-base values)
  sig <- import(bw_file, which = gr, as = "NumericList")[[1]]
  
  tibble(
    position = start:end,
    RPKM = sig
  )
}

# ===============================
# Extract signal for all replicates
# ===============================
all_data <- samples %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("rep"), values_to = "bw") %>%
  filter(!is.na(bw)) %>%
  mutate(data = map(bw, extract_signal)) %>%
  unnest(data)

# ===============================
# Average per Genotype per position
# ===============================
avg_signal <- all_data %>%
  group_by(Genotype, position) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_RPKM = mean(RPKM, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

###---------------------------------------
###Kolmogorov-Smirnov test (KS test)
###---------------------------------------

# Get pDA98 profile
pDA98_vals <- avg_signal %>%
  filter(Genotype == "pDA98") %>%
  pull(mean_RPKM)

# Compare each other genotype to pDA98
genotypes <- setdiff(unique(avg_signal$Genotype), "pDA98")

ks_results <- map_dfr(genotypes, function(g){
  
  vals <- avg_signal %>%
    filter(Genotype == g) %>%
    pull(mean_RPKM)
  
  test <- ks.test(vals, pDA98_vals)
  
  tibble(
    Genotype = g,
    D = test$statistic,
    p_value = test$p.value
  )
})

ks_results
## # A tibble: 3 × 3
##   Genotype     D  p_value
##   <chr>    <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 pDA102   0.762 3.16e- 6
## 2 pDA154   1     3.72e-12
## 3 pDA156   0.619 4.39e- 4
# ===============================
# Define color palette
# ===============================
palette <- c(
  "pDA102" = "black",
  "pDA98"  = "blue",
  "pDA154" = "red",
  "pDA156" = "darkred"
)

# ===============================
# Prepare KS results for plotting
# ===============================
# Add a row for pDA98 itself
ks_results_plot <- ks_results %>%
  bind_rows(tibble(Genotype = "pDA98", D = NA, p_value = NA)) %>%
  # Make a nice label
  mutate(p_label = ifelse(is.na(p_value), "reference", paste0("KS p = ", signif(p_value, 3))))

# ===============================
# Plots with KS p-value in title
# ===============================
plots <- list()
for(g in unique(avg_signal$Genotype)) {
  
  df <- avg_signal %>% filter(Genotype == g)
  ks_label <- ks_results_plot %>% filter(Genotype == g) %>% pull(p_label)
  
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = factor(position), y = mean_RPKM, fill = Genotype)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = palette) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      x = "Genomic position",
      y = "Average RPKM",
      title = paste0("Genotype: ", g, " (", ks_label, ")")
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  
  plots[[g]] <- p
}

# Display plots
plots[["pDA102"]]

plots[["pDA98"]]

plots[["pDA154"]]

plots[["pDA156"]]

# ===============================
# Libraries
# ===============================
library(tidyverse)
library(rtracklayer)
library(GenomicRanges)

# ===============================
# BigWig files (your dataset)
# ===============================
bw_files <- c(
  "SN2.bw","SN3.bw","SN4.bw","SN5.bw",
  "SL2.bw","SL4.bw","SL5.bw",
  "SL12.bw","SL13.bw","SL14.bw","SL15.bw"
)

# ===============================
# Replicate structure
# ===============================
samples <- tribble(
  ~Genotype, ~rep1, ~rep2, ~rep3,
  "pDA98",    "SN2.bw", "SL2.bw", "SL12.bw",
  "pDA102",   "SN3.bw", NA,       "SL13.bw",
  "pDA154",   "SN4.bw", "SL4.bw", "SL14.bw",
  "pDA156",   "SN5.bw", "SL5.bw", "SL15.bw"
)

# ===============================
# Genomic region
# ===============================
chr <- "GL000220.1"
start <- 110315
end   <- 110335

# ===============================
# Function to extract per-base RPKM
# ===============================
extract_signal <- function(bw_file){
  if(is.na(bw_file)) return(NULL)
  
  gr <- GRanges(chr, IRanges(start, end))
  
  # Import as NumericList (per-base values)
  sig <- import(bw_file, which = gr, as = "NumericList")[[1]]
  
  tibble(
    position = start:end,
    RPKM = sig
  )
}

# ===============================
# Extract signal for all replicates
# ===============================
all_data <- samples %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("rep"), values_to = "bw") %>%
  filter(!is.na(bw)) %>%
  mutate(data = map(bw, extract_signal)) %>%
  unnest(data)

# ===============================
# Exclude parental if present
# ===============================
replicate_data <- all_data
# ===============================
# Compute mean RPKM per replicate per subregion
# ===============================

regions <- tibble(
  region_name = c("region1", "region2"),
  start = c(110315, 110326),
  end   = c(110324, 110335)
)

replicate_means <- replicate_data %>%
  # For each region
  group_by(Genotype, bw) %>%
  nest() %>% 
  crossing(regions) %>%    # combine each replicate with each region
  mutate(
    mean_RPKM = map2_dbl(data, region_name, function(df, rname){
      r_start <- regions$start[regions$region_name == rname]
      r_end   <- regions$end[regions$region_name == rname]
      mean(df$RPKM[df$position >= r_start & df$position <= r_end], na.rm=TRUE)
    })
  ) %>%
  select(Genotype, bw, region_name, mean_RPKM) %>%
  arrange(Genotype, bw, region_name)

replicate_means
## # A tibble: 22 × 4
##    Genotype bw      region_name mean_RPKM
##    <chr>    <chr>   <chr>           <dbl>
##  1 pDA102   SL13.bw region1        96940.
##  2 pDA102   SL13.bw region2       376965.
##  3 pDA102   SN3.bw  region1        80273.
##  4 pDA102   SN3.bw  region2       132696.
##  5 pDA154   SL14.bw region1        95471.
##  6 pDA154   SL14.bw region2       180819.
##  7 pDA154   SL4.bw  region1       200199.
##  8 pDA154   SL4.bw  region2      2074294 
##  9 pDA154   SN4.bw  region1        99653.
## 10 pDA154   SN4.bw  region2       146318.
## # ℹ 12 more rows
# ===============================
# Compute percent per replicate
# ===============================
replicate_values <- replicate_means %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = region_name, values_from = mean_RPKM) %>%
  mutate(
    percent = 100 * region1 / (region1 + region2)
  )

# ===============================
# Compute mean ± SEM per Genotype
# ===============================
final_results <- replicate_values %>%
  group_by(Genotype) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_percent = mean(percent, na.rm=TRUE),
    sem_percent  = sd(percent, na.rm=TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ===============================
# Combine RNA helicase groups
# ===============================
replicate_values_grouped <- replicate_values %>%
  mutate(Group = case_when(
    Genotype %in% c("pDA154", "pDA156") ~ "RNA helicase mutants",
    Genotype == "pDA98" ~ "Wildtype",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_  # in case there are other genotypes
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Group))  # exclude any others (like pDA102 if needed)

# ===============================
# Compute mean ± SEM per Group
# ===============================
final_results_grouped <- replicate_values_grouped %>%
  group_by(Group) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_percent = mean(percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    sem_percent  = sd(percent, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ===============================
# t-test between Wildtype and RNA helicase mutants
# ===============================

# Subset the percent values per group
wildtype_vals <- replicate_values_grouped$percent[replicate_values_grouped$Group == "Wildtype"]
mutant_vals   <- replicate_values_grouped$percent[replicate_values_grouped$Group == "RNA helicase mutants"]

# F-test for equal variances
variance_test <- var.test(wildtype_vals, mutant_vals)
variance_test
## 
##  F test to compare two variances
## 
## data:  wildtype_vals and mutant_vals
## F = 0.0019751, num df = 2, denom df = 5, p-value = 0.003945
## alternative hypothesis: true ratio of variances is not equal to 1
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.0002341907 0.0776169706
## sample estimates:
## ratio of variances 
##        0.001975076
t_test_result <- t.test(percent ~ Group,
                        data = replicate_values_grouped,
                        var.equal = FALSE,  # Welch t-test
                        alternative = "less")  # one-sided
t_test_result
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  percent by Group
## t = -2.1944, df = 5.0394, p-value = 0.03962
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group RNA helicase mutants and group Wildtype is less than 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##       -Inf -1.109479
## sample estimates:
## mean in group RNA helicase mutants             mean in group Wildtype 
##                           28.11520                           41.43453
# ===============================
# Define colors
# ===============================
palette_grouped <- c(
  "Wildtype" = "blue",
  "RNA helicase mutants" = "red"
)

# ===============================
# Plot mean + SEM + replicate dots
# ===============================
ggplot() +
  geom_col(data = final_results_grouped,
           aes(x = Group, y = mean_percent, fill = Group),
           width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(data = final_results_grouped,
                aes(x = Group,
                    ymin = mean_percent - sem_percent,
                    ymax = mean_percent + sem_percent),
                width = 0.2,
                size = 0.8) +
  geom_point(data = replicate_values_grouped,
             aes(x = Group, y = percent),
             color = "gray",
             size = 2,
             alpha = 0.6,
             position = position_jitter(width = 0.08)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = palette_grouped) +
  labs(
    x = "Genotype group",
    y = "Relative unmodified (%)",
    title = paste0("t-test p-value = ", signif(t_test_result$p.value, 3))
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14)
